info.name: Pawfront Backend API
info.description:
Complete API testing suite for the Pawfront provider-facing backend.

**Setup**
1. Set the `baseUrl` collection variable (default `https://localhost:5001`).
2. Get a Firebase ID token (see CLAUDE.md `Get a fresh Firebase ID token`) and set `firebaseToken`.
3. Run requests roughly in folder order. Several requests have test scripts that auto-populate ids (`providerAuthIdentityId`, `providerId`, `otpId`, `eventId`, `bookingId`, plus the per-service-type `serviceId*` vars).

**Notes**
- Every endpoint requires the `FirebaseUser` policy → `Authorization: Bearer {{firebaseToken}}`.
- All responses follow the `ApiResponse<T>` envelope: `{ success, data, error }`.
- A provider can register only ONE service category. Pick one of the five service folders and stick to it for a given `providerId`.
- **Per-service flow:** after a provider saves an offering, the server upserts a row in `Provider.ProviderServices` for each sub-offering present (e.g. PetSitter with DayCare + NightStay = two ServiceIds). Closures, bookings, and slot queries are all scoped by `ServiceId`. **Run `3z. Provider Services Catalog → GET /services` after the offering save** — its test script populates `serviceId`, `serviceIdDayCare`, `serviceIdNightStay`, `serviceIdGrooming`, `serviceIdTraining`, `serviceIdVet` for use in the later folders.
- Image-upload requests use multipart with field name `file`.

variable.description: Catch-all: first active ServiceId returned by GET /services. Use when you only care about one service (single-offering categories).
PetSitter DayCare ServiceId. Populated by GET /services if the provider offers DayCare.
PetSitter NightStay ServiceId. Populated by GET /services if the provider offers NightStay.

request.description: Returns the persisted personal information for this provider — name, gender, mobile, date of birth, mobile-verified timestamp, onboarding status, created/updated timestamps. Returns 404 ProviderProfileNotFound when the row is missing.

item.name: 3z. Provider Services Catalog (run after offering save)
item.description: Returns the SQL-backed catalog of services the provider currently offers. Rows are upserted automatically by the offering POSTs (one row per sub-offering present — e.g. PetSitter with DayCare + NightStay = two rows). The test script on `GET /services` populates per-ServiceType collection variables (`serviceIdDayCare`, `serviceIdNightStay`, `serviceIdGrooming`, `serviceIdTraining`, `serviceIdVet`) plus the generic `serviceId` (first active row) for use in the Availability / Closures / Bookings folders below.

request.description: Includes services that were deactivated (the provider removed the matching sub-offering on a later save). Historical closures / bookings can still reference these ServiceIds, so the row is kept.

Slots are computed per-service: capacity, duration rule, partial-day closures, and overlap-with-existing-bookings are all scoped by `serviceId`. For single-offering categories (PetGroomer/PetTrainer/Vet) use `{{serviceId}}` instead of `{{serviceIdDayCare}}`. For PetSitter NightStay use `{{serviceIdNightStay}}`.
Same date, different service — NightStay returns its own slot grid independent of DayCare (different capacity bucket, different duration rule, different closures).

item.name: 5b. Provider Closures (sick leave / vacation) — PER-SERVICE
item.description: Closures are scoped by `ServiceId`. A single POST can close ONE or MANY services at the same time by passing an array of `serviceIds`; the server inserts one closure row per service in a single transaction (all-or-nothing). A closure on DayCare does NOT block NightStay slots/bookings, and vice versa.

item.name: POST close ONE service — DayCare full day

exec: console.warn('Closure batch rejected — bookings exist on one or more services:', json.data.conflictingBookings);

body.raw:
{
  "serviceIds": ["{{serviceIdDayCare}}"],
  "startDate": "2026-06-01",
  "endDate": "2026-06-01",
  "startTime": null,
  "endTime": null,
  "reason": "Sick leave (DayCare only — NightStay remains open)"
}

request.description:
Closes DayCare for the whole day on 2026-06-01. NightStay bookings on the same day are unaffected.

**Response shape (always 200 + success=true):**
- `data.status = "Created"` → `data.closures` is populated (one entry per requested ServiceId).
- `data.status = "BookingsExist"` → no closures were created. `data.conflictingBookings` lists confirmed bookings inside the window across ALL targeted services (each carries its `serviceId`); `data.warningMessage` is the human-readable message. The provider must move/cancel those bookings and retry.

item.name: POST close MULTIPLE services — DayCare + NightStay together (vacation)

request.description: Atomic batch close across two services. Server creates two closure rows (one per ServiceId) in a single transaction. If ANY of the targeted services has a conflicting confirmed booking inside the window, the WHOLE batch is rejected and no rows are inserted.

item.name: POST close single-service provider — generic (PetGroomer / PetTrainer / Vet)

request.description: For categories with only one ServiceType (PetGroomer / PetTrainer / Vet), the generic `{{serviceId}}` collection variable (first active row from `GET /services`) is the easy choice.

item.name: POST close service — partial day (e.g. vet appointment block)

request.description: Partial-day window. StartTime + EndTime must be set together; StartDate must equal EndDate when times are present (multi-day partial windows are rejected with 400 InvalidRequest).

Returns closures whose date range intersects [from, to] across ALL the provider's services. Each row carries `serviceId` so the UI can group them. Both `from` and `to` are optional — omit either for an open-ended side.
Narrow to closures for one specific service.
Reopens a single closure row (one ServiceId). If you closed DayCare + NightStay together as two rows, you must DELETE each closureId to reopen both.

`serviceId` is REQUIRED. The server validates that this ServiceId belongs to the provider and is active (else 400 InvalidServiceId). Capacity is checked per-service — DayCare bookings count separately from NightStay bookings, and closures only block if they target the same ServiceId.

For PetSitter NightStay use `{{serviceIdNightStay}}`. For single-offering categories (PetGroomer / PetTrainer / Vet) use `{{serviceId}}`.

NightStay on the SAME date as DayCare — both can exist independently. (Note: this example uses a NightStay window that crosses midnight; valid only if the provider's weekly availability covers it. Adjust to your provider's hours.)
Returns the full booking history for this provider when no `date` query param is given.

item.name: GET bookings by provider — filter by date

request.description: Returns only the bookings whose `bookingDate` equals the provided `date` (YYYY-MM-DD). Used by the provider's day-view UI.

item.name: 7b. Event Ticket Bookings
item.description:
Ticket purchases against a physical event. **Anyone** signed in can buy tickets for any names — attendee names are free text and not validated against the database. The booking is created with `PaymentStatus = Pending`; flip it to `Paid` (or `Failed`) via the gateway callback request.

Requires the parent `eventId` to be a **Physical** event with a `physical.maximumCapacity` set (Online events return 400 EventNotBookable). Capacity is enforced atomically inside `Event.CreateEventBooking` — concurrent buyers serialise and the (N+1)-th seat returns 409 EventSoldOut.

item.name: POST create event booking (Credit Card, 4 tickets)

request.description:
Creates one booking row + 4 ticket rows in a single race-safe transaction. The booking comes back with `paymentStatus: "Pending"` and the `tickets` array carries one entry per attendee (TicketNumber 1..N).

Response → sets `eventBookingId` collection variable for downstream requests.
Returns the booking envelope + a `tickets` array with one entry per attendee. If 4 tickets were bought, 4 entries come back here.

Simulates the external gateway callback. Flips `PaymentStatus` to `Paid` and records the gateway reference. Idempotent for redelivery of the same (status, reference) pair; a different reference for an already-confirmed booking returns 409 PaymentAlreadyConfirmed.

item.name: 7c. Event Engagement & Organiser Dashboard
item.description:
Two halves of the same dashboard story:

**Public engagement counters** (POST views/shares/inquiries) — anyone signed in can call these from the event detail screen. Each request bumps a single counter on `Event.Events` atomically and returns the updated `{ viewCount, shareCount, inquiryCount }`. Inquiries are a counter only — there is no inquiry content stored today.

**Organiser-only reads** (GET attendees + metrics) — URL-scoped under `/providers/{providerId}/events/{eventId}`. The server verifies the event belongs to that provider; if not it returns 404 EventNotFound (we don't distinguish 'doesn't exist' vs 'not yours' to avoid leaking existence).

request.description: Atomic +1 on `Event.Events.ViewCount`. Call from the mobile event-detail screen. Returns the new `{ viewCount, shareCount, inquiryCount }`.

One row per ticket. Joins booking + ticket; excludes Cancelled bookings; surfaces `paymentStatus` per row so the organiser can chase Pending/Failed payments.

404 EventNotFound if `eventId` doesn't belong to `providerId`.

Returns `{ views, shares, inquiries, confirmedAttendees, earnings }`. `confirmedAttendees` and `earnings` are computed live from confirmed-Paid bookings; the three engagement counters are read from `Event.Events`.

404 EventNotFound if `eventId` doesn't belong to `providerId`.

item.name: POST fetch blob image by URL

request.description:
Universal image fetcher for the Flutter app. Accepts any blob URL that lives under the configured provider-images container and streams the raw image bytes back with the original Content-Type (image/jpeg, image/png, …).

**Response is binary** (not the JSON envelope) — Postman shows it under the response **Preview** tab. Errors still use the envelope:
- 400 InvalidRequest — bad URL, wrong host, or URL not in our container (SSRF guard).
- 404 BlobNotFound — blob does not exist.

Set the `blobUrl` collection variable to the URL returned by any of the upload endpoints (profile/service/event banner).

item.name: 9. Legacy (in-memory)
item.description: The legacy in-memory `/providers/{id}/services` POST/GET endpoints have been REMOVED. `GET /providers/{id}/services` now serves the real SQL-backed catalog — see folder `3z. Provider Services Catalog`. The two legacy `/providers` endpoints below are still in-memory placeholders and don't persist across restarts.
